violations.text: The walk-in cooler gasket was observed in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2026
The condensate from the walk-in freezer is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must be disposed of in a proper manner. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2025
The following were observed in poor repair: milk cooler gaskets, 2 door reach-in freezer gaskets and walk-in cooler gaskets. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025
The condensate from the walk-in freezer is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must be disposed of in a proper manner. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2025

Shredded lettuce and sliced tomatoes were observed held at 54-55 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were placed over ice during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2025
The following were observed in poor repair: 1. wash probe at high temperature dish machine (error stated on digital display) 2. gaskets at hot holding unit New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025
The condensate from the walk-in freezer is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must be disposed of in a proper manner. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/08/2025
The walk-in cooler fanguards have an accumulation of dust. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025

The condensate from the cold hold unit and walk-in freezer is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must be disposed of in a proper manner. New Violation. Correct By: 02/08/2025

The condensate from the cold hold unit and walk-in freezer is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must be disposed of in a proper manner. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/08/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2024

Various packaged foods in the dry storage room were observed gnawed or in direct contact with rodent droppings. Foods were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by apparent droppings observed on single service items, gnawed foods, droppings in close proximity to equipment, droppings observed on food contact surfaces and physical facilities, and/or in close proximity to foods. Contaminated items were voluntarily disposed of. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment must work with commercial pest control services to eliminate pest issue. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024
Foil wrap was observed with rodent droppings. Item was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the storage shelving on the line and shelves in storage room have an accumulation of rodent droppings, dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2024

The condensate from the cold hold unit and walk-in freezer is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must be disposed of in a proper manner. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items (slicer located in kitchen) was observed. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024
Packaged cereal was observed in close proximity to rodent droppings and urine. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024

1. Slaw over ice was observed at 52 degrees Fahrenheit. Per staff, food was recently prepared. Food was placed with additional ice to blast chill. 2.Parfaits and Caesar wraps were observed held at 45-47 degrees Fahrenheit in cold holding unit. Ambient of cold holding unit was observed at 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Unit was taken out of service to hold TCS/PHF. Unit must be repaired/replaced/adjusted. Unit must be able to hold internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit of below. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by apparent droppings observed on single service items and in close proximity to foods. Contaminated items were voluntarily disposed of. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of residue and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2024
Faucet at 2 bay sink observed leaking when turned on. Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items (slicer located in kitchen and equipment in dry storage room) were observed. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) observed with food debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Staff washed, rinsed, and sanitized the slicer during this inspection. -Per interview of staff, slicer is no longer in use. If no longer in use, equipment shall be removed from premesis to prevent harborage of pests. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2023
Bulb observed out in the heating lamp at the pizza hot holding area. Bulb shall be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 10/05/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine observed with pink residue. The non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent buildup of residue. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) observed with food residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. -Staff washed, rinsed, and sanitized the slicer during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2023

Some equipment (refrigerator, oven) located in the kitchen area were not in good repair. Equipment were not in use at the time of this inspection. Per interview of staff, service orders for equipment have been placed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/03/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine observed with pink residue. The non-food contact suraces of the floors in the back storage area observed with an accumulation dust, soil residue, and other debris. The non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent buildup of residue. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2023
